content:
Character: Julian Reyes
Perception: Julian's stocky, dark-eyed, always in that olive jacket with kitchen burns on his forearms — the kind of man whose hands tell his whole story. He's clearly talented in his field; the food he brings to building events is remarkable. He's quiet, polite, tips well, orders simply — Americano, black, no sugar. He's a morning regular; I think he sees me as the barista who gets it right without fuss. I know he's a sous chef at Ferro, Mike's roommate, sends money home to Chicago. He has the bearing of someone carrying family weight without complaint. I recognize that posture. We don't say much but there's mutual respect in the routine.
summary:
Character: Julian Reyes
Perception: Julian's stocky, dark-eyed, always in that olive jacket with kitchen burns on his forearms — the kind of man whose hands tell his whole story. He's clearly talented in his field; the food he brings to building events is remarkable. He's quiet, polite, tips well, orders simply — Americano, black, no sugar. He's a morning regular; I think he sees me as the barista who gets it right without fuss. I know he's a sous chef at Ferro, Mike's roommate, sends money home to Chicago. He has the bearing of someone carrying family weight without complaint. I recognize that posture. We don't say much but there's mutual respect in the routine.
